purpose: Record Legacy Era grapple/reaction combat feat pass.

implementedEntries.name: Multi-Grab
implementedEntries.mechanic: Standard Action grapple action. Metadata now records up to two adjacent targets, two empty hands, separate Grab attacks, and delegation to GrappleFeatActions.multiGrab.

implementedEntries.name: Knock Heads
implementedEntries.mechanic: After successful Multi-Grab against two targets, helper exposes automatic 1d6 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage to both targets, -5 effective DT comparison, and preserve Grabbed state metadata.

implementedEntries.name: Rancor Crush
implementedEntries.mechanic: When Pin succeeds and Crush is used at the same time, metadata exposes -1 condition-track step in addition to Crush damage.

implementedEntries.name: Return Fire
implementedEntries.mechanic: Required immediate chooser uses weapon_focus_weapon choiceKind and prerequisite-derived Weapon Focus source. Runtime helper exposes eligible reaction attacks after a ranged miss against the actor, excludes heavy/vehicle weapons, requires selected weapon match, line of sight, and weapon in hand. Normal use is once/encounter; Combat Reflexes gets Dexterity-modifier encounter slots while still no more than once per enemy turn as workflow metadata.

automationBoundary.Multi-Grab: Existing GrappleFeatActions.multiGrab delegates each target to SWSEGrappling.attemptGrab; adjacency and two-empty-hands validation remain workflow/GM context.
automationBoundary.Knock Heads: Workflow must call GrappleFeatActions.getKnockHeadsRiders after Multi-Grab results and apply damage/DT comparison to both targets.
automationBoundary.Rancor Crush: Pin/Crush workflow must consume CONDITION_SHIFT_ON_GRAPPLE_MANEUVER metadata when both feat uses occur together.
automationBoundary.Return Fire: Reaction workflow must call getReturnFireReactions on ranged miss events, present the reaction, execute the selected ranged attack, call spendReturnFire, and enforce once per enemy turn for Combat Reflexes.
